Conditions at Playa Hermosa

Live data from the Open-Meteo forecast grid for the property coordinates. Wet season runs May through November — expect warm mornings, afternoon convective showers, and intense greenery through the dry-forest transition.

Wet Season (May–November) — Guanacaste's Pacific coast receives the great majority of its annual rainfall as afternoon and evening convective downpours, typically following warm, clear mornings. Ocean swells are more variable; check tide tables before beach access or kayak launches.
Tides · SINAMOT Reference Station

The tides of Playa Hermosa

June–December 2026 data transcribed from the SINAMOT printed tables (Universidad Nacional de Costa Rica). Reference station: Puntarenas / Nacascolo. Times CST (UTC−6), heights in metres.

Full tide chart →
—

Reading the Tables

Playa Hermosa has a mixed semidiurnal pattern — two highs and two lows per day of unequal heights. The higher of the two daily highs typically occurs in the morning during the dry season, shifting to afternoon through the wet months.

Best Intertidal Access

For rocky intertidal exploration, target the lowest low tides — typically 0.15–0.25m in the chart. New and full moon periods produce the greatest tidal range. The full tide chart flags the five lowest days in each month.

Wet Season Caution

June–October swells from Pacific storm systems can combine with high tides to push surf well up the beach. Evening high tides during the wet season should be approached with care, particularly when tropical weather systems are active.

Condominio El Oasis  ·  Playa Hermosa, Guanacaste, Costa Rica
